---
title: "Formatting Test"
description: "A temporary note for checking the notes archive and article layout."
date: 2026-07-14
---

{{< include /_macros.qmd >}}

## A short calculation

This temporary note is published to check the notes archive, typography,
table of contents, and mathematical typesetting.

Let $X$ be a square-integrable random variable. Its variance is

$$
\operatorname{Var}(X) = \E[X^2] - \E[X]^2.
$$ {#eq-variance}

@eq-variance is nonnegative by the Cauchy--Schwarz inequality.

::: {#thm-expectation-bound}

## Expectation bound

For any $X \in L^2$,

$$
\abs{\E[X]} \le \sqrt{\E[X^2]}.
$$

:::

::: {.proof}

Apply Cauchy--Schwarz to the inner product of $X$ and the constant random
variable $1$.

:::
